title: erysimum asperum var. elatum
intro: Erysimum asperum var. elatum, commonly known as tall wallflower, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States. It is a member of the Brassicaceae family and is known for its tall spikes of yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Erysimum asperum var. elatum can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
